title: README AI-executable runbook
qaNote: All 5 ACs verified by grep commands; ordered runbook, Copilot note, no-Node fallback, package.json explanation, and AI framing all present and correct.
userStory: As a non-technical user setting up the Personal OS, I want to show the README to my AI assistant and say 'set this up for me', so that the assistant runs every prerequisite step and opens the wizard without me needing to know npm commands.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The README's setup section contains numbered, copy-runnable steps in this exact order: (1) check/install Node.js ≥ 20, (2) `npm install --prefix work-hq`, (3) `npm run onboard` — with each step on its own numbered line and no inter-step dependency left implicit. Verified by: `grep -n 'npm install --prefix work-hq' README.md` returns a match.
The README explicitly states that the as-is configuration assumes GitHub Copilot in the VS Code native chat. `grep -n 'assumes GitHub Copilot\|as-is configuration assumes' README.md` returns a match.

The README documents the chat-only os-helper Butler path as the no-Node fallback with this instruction or equivalent: 'If you don't have Node.js, open this folder in VS Code and say "onboard me" to your assistant.' `grep -n 'onboard me\|no-Node\|without Node' README.md` returns a match.

The README contains one line explaining that the repo-root `package.json` exists solely to launch the Work HQ and that the OS works without it. `grep -n 'package.json' README.md` returns a match on that explanatory line.
The setup section is framed as AI-assistant-executable: an explicit instruction such as 'point your AI assistant at this README and say: set this up for me' appears before the numbered steps.

technicalNotes: Edit `README.md` at the repo root. This is a documentation-only story — no app code, no skill changes. Framing: lead the setup section with the AI-assistant invocation line. Ordered steps must be copy-runnable commands (not prose descriptions). Copilot assumption note and adaptation guidance per OQ-003 resolution. No-Node fallback documents the os-helper 'onboard me' trigger per ADR-P6-004 consequences.
outOfScope: Root package.json creation or modification (implementation concern), Work HQ app code changes, skill file edits, any other README section beyond the setup/onboarding section.

verificationPlan.checks: Returns a match confirming the exact install command is present in the README runbook.

verificationPlan.command: grep -n 'set this up for me\|point your AI' README.md
verificationPlan.checks: Returns a match confirming the AI-assistant-executable framing appears before the numbered steps.

perAc.ac: AC1 — Numbered, copy-runnable steps in exact order (Node ≥ 20, npm install --prefix work-hq, npm run onboard) each on its own numbered line with no implicit inter-step dependency
perAc.evidence: ran `grep -n 'npm install --prefix work-hq' README.md` → line 34: '2. **Install Work HQ dependencies:** `npm install --prefix work-hq`'; diff confirms lines 33-35 carry the three steps in order: (1) `node --version` / install LTS, (2) `npm install --prefix work-hq`, (3) `npm run onboard`; step 1 says 'before continuing' making the dependency explicit; ran `grep -n 'npm run onboard' README.md` → line 35 confirmed

perAc.ac: AC2 — README explicitly states as-is configuration assumes GitHub Copilot in VS Code native chat
perAc.evidence: ran `grep -n 'assumes GitHub Copilot\|as-is configuration assumes' README.md` → line 27: 'The as-is configuration assumes GitHub Copilot in the VS Code native chat. If you use a different assistant, your AI can adapt the steps to match your tool.' Satisfies OQ-003 resolution verbatim.

perAc.ac: AC3 — Chat-only os-helper 'onboard me' path documented as no-Node fallback
perAc.evidence: ran `grep -n 'onboard me\|no-Node\|without Node' README.md` → line 37: '> **No-Node fallback.** If you don't have Node.js, open this folder in VS Code and say **"onboard me"** to your assistant. The `os-helper` skill runs a chat-only onboarding — no install required.' Cross-checked: `grep -n 'onboard me' skills/os-helper/SKILL.md` → line 24 confirms 'onboard me' is an explicit trigger in the skill file — no mismatch.

perAc.ac: AC4 — One explanatory line stating repo-root package.json exists solely to launch Work HQ and OS works without it
perAc.evidence: ran `grep -n 'package.json' README.md` → line 39: '**About `package.json`:** The repo-root `package.json` exists solely to launch the Work HQ and run the onboarding wizard — the OS itself is plain markdown files and works without it.' Single match, satisfies 'one line' requirement.

perAc.ac: AC5 — AI-assistant-executable framing ('point your AI assistant at this README and say: set this up for me') appears before the numbered steps
perAc.evidence: ran `grep -n 'set this up for me\|point your AI' README.md` → line 25: 'Point your AI assistant at this README and say: **"set this up for me"**'; numbered steps begin at line 33 — framing is 8 lines before the runbook.

qaReport.securityAndMigrationGates: Secrets scan: PASS — diff contains no credentials, tokens, API keys, database URLs, or private key material. Migration check: N/A — documentation-only change, no schema modifications.
qaReport.riskNotes: The two path corrections in the same commit (.github/skills/ → skills/ in the How It Works diagram and Directory Map) are factually correct: the skills/ directory exists at the repo root and the .github/skills/ files are deleted per git status. These corrections touch no AC-responsible content and do not affect any acceptance criterion. The npm run onboard script exists in the root package.json ('onboard': 'npm --prefix work-hq run onboard'), confirming the runbook step is not a dead reference.
qaReport.recommendedFollowUps: The work-hq/ onboard script target should be smoke-tested in a future story to confirm the end-to-end path from 'npm run onboard' to the wizard launching works for a net-new clone — this is explicitly out of scope for story 007 but is the next integration risk in the runbook.

impl.notes: Documentation-only story. Added the 'Setting Up — Ask Your AI Assistant' section to README.md immediately before the existing 'Quick Start' section. The section contains: (1) AI-assistant-executable framing ('point your AI assistant at this README and say: set this up for me'); (2) Copilot assumption statement ('as-is configuration assumes GitHub Copilot in the VS Code native chat'); (3) numbered runbook with Node ≥ 20 check, npm install --prefix work-hq, and npm run onboard; (4) no-Node fallback with 'onboard me' trigger; (5) explanatory line about repo-root package.json. The diff also contains two pre-existing working-tree corrections (.github/skills/ → skills/) in the How It Works diagram and Directory Map sections that were already modified before this story began — they are factually correct and included in the same commit.
